name: the --project flag brings the project working-overlay namespaces into scope and reports them
command: ID=FEAT-560C; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/acquire-technology-rules.sh" --technology Vue --project $ID --source-file g.txt --source-id sid --now 2026-07-05T00:00:00Z >/dev/null 2>&1; R=$(bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/full-surface-intake.sh" --workload "a platform" --project $ID --classify 2>/dev/null | ruby -rjson -e 'd=JSON.parse(STDIN.read); puts(d["project_overlay_namespaces"]==["vue"] && d["workload_classification"]["namespaces"].include?("vue") ? "ok":"no")'); rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards"/_project/$ID; [ "$R" = ok ] && echo overlay-namespaces-in-scope 1>&2
setup: printf '%s\n' 'Always bind a unique key to v-for.' > g.txt
